title: Support shortname resolution for artifacts
body:
Pulling artifact doesn't support shortname resolution, which is supported for images.

```
❯ podman artifact pull crio/artifact:singlefile
Error: initializing source docker://crio/artifact:singlefile: reading manifest singlefile in docker.io/crio/artifact: requested access to the resource is denied

❯ podman pull crio/busybox
Trying to pull quay.io/crio/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a3ed95caeb02 done
Copying blob f70adabe43c0 skipped: already exists
Writing manifest to image destination
8ac48589692a53a9b8c2d1ceaa6b402665aa7fe667ba51ccc03002300856d8c7
```

The registries.conf is 
```toml
unqualified-search-registries = ["quay.io"]
```

The repository is available.
https://quay.io/organization/crio

version
```
❯ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.7.0-dev
API Version:  5.7.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.25.3
Git Commit:   24872421a894f9ffde40fdc256c6886bc732b982
Built:        Mon Oct 20 16:18:46 2025
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

container-libs version were:
```
	go.podman.io/common v0.65.1-0.20251016162239-c4c5e00ad22d
	go.podman.io/image/v5 v5.37.1-0.20251016133615-aa970d2c7532
	go.podman.io/storage v1.60.1-0.20251016133615-aa970d2c7532
```

Looking at the code, the image resolution code is implemented in common ([code](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/feb36e4fe672f899303f8aeddf7663cf70d2b17b/vendor/go.podman.io/common/libimage/pull.go#L559)), so will the one for artifact be implemented in common?
Let me know if I should open an issue in c/podman repo.

comments.body: Short names are generally a very bad idea, and IIRC it was an intentional decision not to repeat the mistake for artifacts. Cc: @Luap99 @baude .
yeah I tend to agree we should not do shortnames but I am aware of explicit design discussions around it

That said not doing shortnames also makes the UI much more confusing for users as some commands resolve the names and others not.

Also unqualified-search-registries and short name aliases are two different things, I assume we support neither? Should we support the short name aliases?

Aliases are primarily an interactive feature (with interactive prompts and users’ choices stored into per-user state); CRI-O can’t use that. Although it _is_ possible to ship a set of pre-defined aliases in a system-wide config file ( https://github.com/containers/shortnames ) and IIRC OpenShift does ship _some_ configuration of that kind.

Aliases are much better than search because they are not _ambiguous_, but there can still be conflicts (“to install our software, set the `mysql` alias to `registry.vendor.example/our-patched-version/mysql`” from two different vendors).

And, relevant for OpenShift, the only existing credential passing mechanism (to my knowledge) from users’ namespaces to CRI-O does not work with short names at all (compare the extremely horrible warning in https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/openshift_container_platform/4.19/html/images/image-configuration-classic#images-configuration-shortname_image-configuration — basically any time the user references a short name, CRI-O receives, and will try to use, `docker.io` credentials with that short name). There were vague plans to fix that approximately forever, and it never happened — so I think adding any more short-name supporting features to Kubernetes is irresponsible.

If users want artifacts, it’s by definition a new application / deployment, so let them do the work to work with fully-qualified names only.

latest tag is tacked in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27083

The digest likely deserve its own bug, that should work I assume
Thanks I opened #408 and closing this one.

Sorry, let me reopen this issue, because I have something I want to confirm.

This command successfully pulled nginx image. I don't set no nginx aliases in the config.
Is this, resolving to docker.io by default, intentional as well?
```
❯ podman artifact pull nginx
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 80bfbb8a41a2 done   |
Copying config 0ed463b26d done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
```
According to the log, it seems it uses `docker.io/library/nginx`.
```
DEBU[0000] Trying to access "docker.io/library/nginx:latest"
```
Apparently, any images are resolved to `docker.io/library/<name>`.
```
❯ podman artifact pull aaaaaaaa
Error: initializing source docker://aaaaaaaa:latest: reading manifest latest in docker.io/library/aaaaaaaa: requested access to the resource is denied
```

also this artifact can be specified only with `nginx` not `docker.io/library/nginx`.

```
❯ podman artifact inspect nginx
{
...
}
```

```
❯ podman artifact inspect docker.io/library/nginx
Error: docker.io/library/nginx: artifact does not exist
```

Hum… with my c/image hat on, I’m fine with short names _always, deterministically_, resolving to `docker.io[/library]`. There is no ambiguity.

As for whether c/common or Podman should enforce users writing a fully-qualified name and avoid this special case, I don’t have a strong opinion.

**But** what is the CRI-O impact? The “image volume” pulls happen through the long-standing CRI-O “PullImage” API, don’t they? In that case Kubelet ~correctly finds the `docker.io` credentials, and correctly passes them to CRI-O, so that works — but CRI-O’s `PullImage` is implemented to interpret short names as a search, consuming the credentials incorrectly again. And only within the short-name-search loop it finds that the input might be an artifact and switches to an artifact-pulling backend.

So, for CRI-O, I think it’s still desirable to prohibit short names, and that implies that they should be prohibited for Podman, and in the c/common shared code, as well. @containers/podman-maintainers ?
I was talking with @baude today, I think the consensus is do not allow any short names whatsoever so we should enforce that.

> But what is the CRI-O impact?

There's not that big impact. I opened this issue because I thought artifacts should have the same behavior as images.
kubelet doesn't resolve unqualified names, but CRI-O resolves those before passing the name to the pull function. We can resolve the name if container-libs doesn't. (we may be going to remove unqualified resolution for artifacts though).

> This would become a type change, however, so would everyone be cool with that ?

+1 for implementing `ArtifactReference`. I think that would fill the UI gaps between images and artifacts.

cc @saschagrunert
I am fine with the library enforcing this, I would like the podman CLI to handle at least default tag of :latest if not specified.  Or throw an error on empty tag.
Since the shortname resolution is not supported by design, I'm closing this issue.
